dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6299 IH: Removing Insurance Gaps for Health Treatment (RIGHT) Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Fulcher introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act to ensure the appropriate availability of short-term limited duration insurance.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Removing Insurance Gaps for Health Treatment (RIGHT) Act of 2025.

section.header: Ensuring the appropriate availability of short-term limited duration insurance
section.text: Section 2791(b) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300gg–91(b)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The term short-term limited duration insurance means health insurance coverage provided under a contract with a health insurance issuer that—

subparagraph.text: has an expiration date specified in the contract that is less than 12 months after the original effective date of the contract; and
has a duration of not more than 3 years (taking into account renewals or extensions) after the original effective date of the contract.
